title: Standing registry-drift bijection test: helper runtime verb lists <-> command-registry entries
action: Per DA5 (decision 30606de4), add a standing test to test_bee_cli.mjs that makes registry drift a red test instead of a silent gap (this exact gap shipped in the PR: cells.update existed on the helper but not in the registry). Derive each helper's verb list from RUNTIME BEHAVIOR, never by grepping helper source (critical pattern 20260710: a drift guard grepping a module's own source pins syntax, and pinned syntax can be the bug): invoke each of bee_cells.mjs, bee_reservations.mjs, bee_decisions.mjs with a deliberately unknown command (inside the suite's existing temp-repo fixture — helpers refuse to run outside a bee repo root, proven in validation) and parse the verb list from the contract line 'Unknown command ... Use: v1, v2, ...' the helper already emits. TWO PARSE TRAPS (plan-checker verified): (t1) the contract line ends with a period, so the last verb parses as 'judge.'/'sweep.'/'search.' unless the trailing period is stripped; (t2) bee_cells.mjs also emits a SECOND, flag-level 'Use: --id ID --file ...' line from its update verb — anchor the parse strictly on the line that starts with 'Unknown command', never on any other 'Use:' occurrence. Assert set equality BOTH directions against COMMAND_REGISTRY: (a) every runtime verb has a registry entry named `<group>.<verb>`, (b) every registry entry named `<group>.*` corresponds to a runtime verb, (c) the only dot-free entry is 'status' and every entry's group is one of status|cells|reservations|decisions. Failure messages must name the missing/extra verb and which side owns it (helper or registry) so the fix cell is obvious.

truths: removing the cells.update registry entry (thought experiment, not committed) would turn this test red with a message naming cells.update as present-on-helper/missing-in-registry
the test derives verbs from helper runtime output only — no helper source file is read or grepped by the test
test runs green inside the suite on this repo
prohibitions: no grep/readFile over the 4 helper sources inside the new test

trace.outcome: Added DA5 registry<->helper-verb bijection test to test_bee_cli.mjs: derives cells/reservations/decisions verb lists from each helper's runtime 'Unknown command ... Use: ...' stderr line (never by grepping helper source), asserts set equality both directions against COMMAND_REGISTRY, and a dot-free-entry==status-only check. Thought-experiment proof: temporarily removing cells.update from the registry (not committed, restored byte-identical) turned the test red naming cells.update as present-on-helper/missing-in-registry.
